description: Fix animation and interaction issues in vant-lynx components based on root cause analysis

issue1_loading_spinner.rootCause: Lynx does not support CSS @keyframes animation. Current implementation is a static border ring.
issue1_loading_spinner.solution: Implement rotation using JS-driven animation (CSS transitions with setInterval, or worklet)

issue2_popup_interaction.symptom: Popup and ActionSheet overlay click does not close them
issue2_popup_interaction.rootCause: vue-lynx vShow directive does not integrate with <Transition>. When show becomes false, vShow immediately sets display:none without waiting for leave animation. This is a vue-lynx runtime bug, but we can work around it in components.
issue2_popup_interaction.solution: Use v-if instead of v-show for Transition children, or manage visibility manually with CSS opacity/pointer-events instead of display:none
issue2_popup_interaction.workaround: For now, avoid relying on v-show + Transition. Use conditional rendering (v-if) or manual visibility control.

userStories.title: Loading component implements rotation animation
userStories.description: Replace static border ring with rotating animation using JS-driven approach (CSS transition + setInterval or timer-based class toggling)
acceptanceCriteria: Animation should be smooth (at least 30fps equivalent)

userStories.title: Popup uses opacity-based visibility instead of v-show
userStories.description: Replace v-show with manual visibility control using opacity and pointer-events. This avoids the vShow + Transition bug.
acceptanceCriteria: Popup opens and closes smoothly with animation
Transition classes still apply for enter/leave animations
No reliance on v-show for visibility

userStories.title: Overlay uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: Replace v-show with opacity-based visibility control to work around vShow + Transition bug

userStories.title: Dialog uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: Apply same fix as Popup - use opacity/pointer-events instead of v-show

userStories.title: Toast uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: Apply same fix as Popup - use opacity/pointer-events instead of v-show

userStories.title: Notify uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: Apply same fix - replace v-show with opacity control

userStories.title: ActionSheet inherits Popup fix
userStories.description: ActionSheet wraps Popup, so fixing Popup should fix ActionSheet. Verify it works.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting an action works and closes sheet

userStories.title: ShareSheet inherits Popup fix
userStories.description: ShareSheet wraps Popup, verify it works after Popup fix

userStories.title: ImagePreview uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: ImagePreview likely uses v-show + Transition, apply same fix

userStories.title: DropdownMenu uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: Dropdown items likely use v-show, apply fix

userStories.title: Popover uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: Apply same fix to Popover component

userStories.title: SwipeCell uses opacity-based visibility for actions
userStories.description: SwipeCell reveal actions may use v-show, verify and fix if needed

userStories.title: Collapse uses opacity-based visibility
userStories.description: Collapse items expand/collapse animation, check if v-show is used

technicalNotes.workaroundPattern: Instead of <Transition><view v-show="show">...</view></Transition>, use: <view :style="{ opacity: show ? 1 : 0, pointerEvents: show ? 'auto' : 'none', transition: 'opacity 0.3s ease' }">...</view>. For enter/leave class-based animations, manually toggle classes using watch() and setTimeout().
technicalNotes.loadingRotation: Use setInterval to rotate by toggling transform: rotate(Ndeg) every frame. Or use CSS transition on transform with a timer updating the rotation value.
technicalNotes.futureNote: The proper fix is in vue-lynx runtime - integrating vShow with Transition. But that's a larger change. These component-level workarounds are sufficient for now.
